subject: Revert "[OOM] Automate certificate generation for CMPv2 Cert Service"
commit_message:
Revert "[OOM] Automate certificate generation for CMPv2 Cert Service"

This reverts commit 8f0b6f6e7a3a231558343b7dba0b06c0c1228527.

Reason for revert: This change breaks one of the ONAP Architecture Principles, namely "4.4.7 Runtime Independence from Internet" -> https://wiki.onap.org/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=84642385

As a result the deployment fails with "Unable to find image 'openjdk:11-jre-slim' locally" due to dynamic spawning of docker container in makefile based on image not defined in OOM helm chart
s.

Change-Id: I58ea0d62f2c1c76abb6df36c347f74f4abf4e950
Signed-off-by: Bartek Grzybowski <b.grzybowski@partner.samsung.com>
Issue-ID: OOM-2588

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 2 on the queue.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/5932/

Patch Set 1:

While I understand that this change was a leap forward in right direction I'd like you to consider changing the implementation as the current one, as I underlined in commit msg, breaks one of the architectural principles and fails onap deployment. It went unnoticed in any of integration testing as they are performed online which is not entirely "target" deployment model for ONAP. The bug was discovered in Samsung lab where we do perform deployment in offline manner.

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab Orange 1: Chained-CI build started, max time: 14400 seconds

Patch Set 1:

Instead of this change I propose to use any ONAP image to perform keytool and openssl operations.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/5932/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/5932

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Instead of this change I propose to use any ONAP image to perform keytool and openssl operations.

Yes, sure, I'm not insisting on reverting if a better solution will be provided soon, just wanted to drag everyone's attention to the issue.

Just please beware that your proposal has the side effect that should that image, that would get/already is defined in one of the OOM charts, be a subject to change (removal, tag change) the cert mgmt mechanism will again fail in offline deployment. So there would have to be some kind of sync mechanism between what's defined in OOM charts in scope of images and here in the docker cmd spawning the container based on that image.

So there would have to be some kind of sync mechanism between what's defined in OOM charts in scope of images and here in the docker cmd spawning the container based on that image.
> 
> Hi Bartek, I'm aware about this. I don't know exactly how offline installer works but I suspect that a crawler extracts list of ONAP images from some place. Do you know from what place exactly?

You are correct. We have a script for that which is a part of toolchain for building a deployment package: https://git.onap.org/oom/offline-installer/tree/build/creating_data/docker-images-collector.sh

What it does is compiling charts (make) and then simply parses the output of resolved helm templates, see line #63.

But please do not consider this whole issue from Samsung's deployment model, I just mentioned it for reference. The real issue with this change is that it's against some architectural concepts (which offline-installer merely follows) which I believe we ought to follow.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Hi all,
> Is the plan to revert the commit or to "repair" the function?

Hi Sylvain,

After brainstorm session following options popped up:
1. Revert and use hardcoded certs till we can use Cert-Manager's internal issuer in next (Honolulu?) release to enroll certificates
2. Extend offline installer with list of utility images and add missing one to such list
3. Use of any image which is already used by ONAP (e.g. nexus3.onap.org:10001/onap/integration-java11:7.1.0)

We haven't decided yet which option we will take that's why I asked Bartek to create bug report.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > Hi all,
> > Is the plan to revert the commit or to "repair" the function?
> 
> Hi Sylvain,
> 
> After brainstorm session following options popped up:
> 1. Revert and use hardcoded certs till we can use Cert-Manager's internal issuer in next (Honolulu?) release to enroll certificates
> 2. Extend offline installer with list of utility images and add missing one to such list
> 3. Use of any image which is already used by ONAP (e.g. nexus3.onap.org:10001/onap/integration-java11:7.1.0)
> 
> We haven't decided yet which option we will take that's why I asked Bartek to create bug report.

2. I again repeat that this is not offline-installer issue. If someone creates some other deployment module to work offline in real production deployment it will still fail there.

3.Is this image used in any chart?:
oom$ git grep integration-java11
oom$
Event if you use an image defined in one of charts, cert deployment will fail after that image tag is changed unless you'll be bumping that tag in cert service as well which is hard to maintain I think.

Hi @Sylvain, @Krzysztof;
I have corrected yesterday typo in option #4...

(NEW) We can leverage Java installed on node from which ONAP is installed - @Sylvain, @Krzysztof: would you accept such dependency?
Official ONAP installation guide suggests to use "first controller as it has also access to internal Kubernetes services, which can be convenient" to install ONAP, which is spawned from Ubuntu 18.04 image.

Hi,
RKE is not mandated to install ONAP so I'm not really fan of this...
For integration-java11, it's used (underneath) by nbi but we don't retrieve the tag.

I would personnally prefer to add the needed container image name "somehow" to the mandated list of image that must be downloaded if offline

let's wait @Krzsysztof being back to see his thoughts

Hmmm how about if we introduce a fake deployment file?

offline installer already sets a fake masterPassword so if we do:

fake_deployment.yaml
  {{ if eq .Values.global.masterPassword "TemplatePassword" }}
    some deployment with the image that you are using
  {{ end }}


This way offline installer would always collect the right file but this would never get deployed.

This would be a quick fix but as a proper fix I'd suggest that offline installer should set a global flag while running a helm template ie:

global.offlineInstallerTemplating

and then we could use this to both fake master password instead of setting it explicitly in the offline installer but also use it here in this fake deployment

What do you thing guys>?

Patch Set 2:

> I would personnally prefer to add the needed container image name "somehow" to the mandated list of image that must be downloaded if offline

> and then we could use this to both fake master password instead of setting it explicitly in the offline installer but also use it here in this fake deployment

How would you like to include those in ONAP architectural requirements? Because that document and not Offline-installer describes the concepts for the platform. Besides - allowing one exception from specification is a dangerous precedent that anyone in the future will be adducing to justify other "WOs".

Hmmm then where is the offline installer current architecture and processing documented?

BTW
Having a flag which informs the source code which "compiler" is processing the source code is a common practice.

Take a look at gcc and g++ and a typical ifndef in the source code to use different constructions depending if we are building with C or C++ compiler

Here we would use the same concept and just define a flag if the source code is being process by the offline istaller

> Hmmm then where is the offline installer current architecture and processing documented?

It don't see how Your comment relates to my statements but please consider the fact that offline-installer implementation is in no way __reference__ implementation for ONAP offline deployment which seems to be silent assumption in this discussion.

Instead we should completely forget it and focus on avoiding introducing any kind of runtime dependencies. If this requirement is no longer valid please escalate it to PTL to remove point 4.4.7 from arch principles.

4.4.7 says "ONAP must be runnable in a completely private environment with no routing to Internet. This effectively means that any runtime downloading of libraries, definitions and resources from the internet is prohibited and everything must be packaged to the images of the ONAP installation package."

In particular it doesn't say "...everything must be packaged to the images of the ONAP installation package _unless_ there is some fancy workaround in the code for online parts which offline deployment modules can hook into".

Ok awesome but before we get to installing onap we need:

BIOS/UEFI
Kernel
Init
Whole bunch of userspace daemons
Kubernetes
Helm
Helm deploy plugin etc
make and a few other tools

Does this "offline principle" apply also to those components? I don't thing so. It's all a matter of discussion where is the border between ONAP and underlying infra.

In my opinion everything that happens before ONAP containers start is not a part of ONAP it's "underlying infra". Then this makefile that creates certs is not a part of ONAP or we can just modify the makefile and just allow user to choose if he or she wants to "refresh" artifacts or not
